response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, Java, C#) and notable low-latency systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust expertise, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no Rust experience listed. Geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map is a strong positive match, but the core language requirement gap and a current career gap (caregiving since October 2025) make this a borderline case worth a screening call rather than a confident yes.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP req/sec
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, fluent across multiple languages and stacks
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience listed; C/C++ work is over a decade old and limited in scope
Recent career gap (caregiving) since October 2025 may affect availability
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not the systems-level languages the role centers on
